jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening sequence car crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation after mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase with perfect character integration and economy, though routine for the series

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Oh, no, Mrs. Krabappel. If I don't get a 'C' average, my dad won't let me go to Kamp Krusty. Well, it isn't fair to the other children, but all right.
jokes.setup: Bart desperately needs better grades for camp
jokes.punchline: Teacher caves immediately despite acknowledging unfairness
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing both Bart's manipulation and Krabappel's weakness

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Mrs. Krabappel, I really need to go to camp this summer. Could you help me get my grades up?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Bart, that's not fair to the other students who actually do their work.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I know, but—

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Oh, forget it. You're an A student.

jokes.text: Much obliged, doll!
jokes.setup: Getting grade changed inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Bart responds like a 1940s gangster
jokes.explanation: Unexpected voice choice for a 10-year-old creates good character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: See, I'm gonna level with ya, teach. You're gonna change that F to an A, see? And nobody's gotta know about it. We can work this out nice and quiet-like, or we can do this the hard way. What's it gonna be, huh? You scratch my back, I scratch yours. That's how things work in this man's world, see?

jokes.text: Attention, everyone. This is Principal Skinner. I trust you all remembered to bring in your implements of destruction. Now, let's trash this dump.
jokes.setup: Authority figure making announcement
jokes.punchline: Principal encouraging students to destroy school
jokes.explanation: Subverts authority figure expectations with absurd reversal of school rules

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Attention students, I have an announcement. Due to budget cuts, we can no longer afford to maintain this building. So I'm authorizing you all to destroy the school.

jokes.text: Wake up, boy. [Groans, Gasps] I dreamt it was the last day of school. Well, it is. Oh, how do I know this isn't some beautiful dream too?
jokes.setup: Bart thinks he's dreaming about school ending
jokes.punchline: Questions if reality itself might be a dream
jokes.explanation: Decent philosophical humor but not particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, if I'm dreaming about school ending, what if everything is a dream? What if reality itself is just a dream and I'm going to wake up and find out I'm actually a giant butterfly?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't be ridiculous, boy. If you were a butterfly, you'd have much better hair.

jokes.text: You know, a pinch is more traditional. [Loud Gobbling, Belching]
jokes.setup: Homer kicks Bart to prove he's awake
jokes.punchline: Bart suggests pinching while Homer makes disgusting noises
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor with typical Homer grossness

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Why don't you just pinch yourself?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Good idea!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *makes disgusting pinching and groaning noises*

jokes.text: Sure do! When you're 18, you're out the door!
jokes.setup: Marge asks if Homer remembers his promise to the children
jokes.punchline: Homer recalls wrong promise about kicking kids out
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection that reveals Homer's parenting priorities

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, do you remember your promise to the children?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, I promised I'd kick them out of the house when they turned eighteen.

jokes.text: Remember, when you see my report card, they got this new grading system this year. It now goes, 'D,' 'B,' 'A,' 'C.'
jokes.setup: Bart setting up excuse for bad grades
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake grading system that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart scheme with absurd internal logic that fits his character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, the grades don't mean anything. My teacher's using some new grading system where an 'F' stands for 'Fantastic' and a 'D' means 'Dynamite.' So really, I'm doing great!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's my boy! Straight A's!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, those are F's and D's.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! F for Fantastic!

jokes.text: If you want something in this life, you have to work for it. Now, quiet! They're about to announce the lottery numbers.
jokes.setup: Homer giving life lesson about working hard
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts it by focusing on lottery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer hypocrisy with immediate contradiction that reveals his true character

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Work hard, kids. That's the key to success.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You gotta apply yourself, study, practice...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, hold on. Lotto tickets are half off at the Kwik-E-Mart.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Kids, forget everything I just said. We're gonna be rich!

jokes.text: [Man] Seventeen, 32-- D'oh! five-- D'oh! eight-- Whoo-hoo! 47. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer listening to lottery numbers
jokes.punchline: Gets excited at one number but disappointed by all others
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pattern with Homer's signature reactions

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ooh! Seven!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: ...Three. Aw.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: ...Twelve. D'oh!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: ...Forty-two. Why me?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: ...Six. Woo-hoo! We won! ...The lottery commission regrets to inform you that you also need the powerball number.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Thirty-six, 24, 36. Ha, ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Skinner clearing out lockers
jokes.punchline: Reciting measurements like describing a woman's figure
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that feels somewhat forced and out of character

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: 36-24-36, what an hourglass figure!

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Wait, these are locker measurements.

jokes.text: I've never gotten a 'B' before. How could this have happened? I feel so dirty!
jokes.setup: Lisa discovers a B on her report card
jokes.punchline: Treats it like moral corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - perfectionist treating minor flaw as catastrophe

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A B? A B?! Oh, this is a disaster! My entire future is ruined!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Lisa, it's just one B. It's not the end of the world.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Not the end of the world? Mom, I've become a C student. Next thing you know, I'll be shoplifting and taking drugs!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, and then you'll be like your old man. I got a D in high school!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Exactly! See? This is how it starts!

jokes.text: The dirt's not coming off! [Panting]
jokes.setup: Lisa feeling 'dirty' from getting a B
jokes.punchline: Literally trying to scrub herself clean
jokes.explanation: Good visual extension of the metaphor with Lisa's neurotic behavior

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I got a B. A B! I feel so dirty.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I need to scrub myself clean.

jokes.text: Now, Lisa, everyone needs a blotch on their permanent record. Perhaps I'm not making myself clear. [Hysterical Giggle] I think you should reconsider. Lisa, you're hurting me!
jokes.setup: Lisa politely questioning her grade
jokes.punchline: Escalates to physical intimidation of teacher
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Lisa's perfectionism becoming threatening

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Miss Hoover, I was wondering if you could explain why I received a C on my essay?

dialogue.speaker: Miss Hoover
dialogue.line: Well, Lisa, your thesis was unclear.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Unclear? I worked very hard on that essay.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Hoover
dialogue.line: I'm sure you did, but a C is a fair grade.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A fair grade? Miss Hoover, I demand an explanation!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm going to stand right here until you change it.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: And if you don't, I'll... I'll report you to Principal Skinner!

jokes.text: I'd also like to add, as I gaze upon your beauty... I've never seen an angel fly so low. Forget it, short pants.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to charm his teacher with compliments
jokes.punchline: Teacher shuts him down with harsh nickname
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic between characters with Bart's failed manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Mrs. Krabappel, has anyone ever told you that you have the most radiant smile? And those eyes—they sparkle like diamonds.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Oh Bart, that's sweet, but we both know you're just trying to get out of trouble.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: No way! I'm serious. You're like the most beautiful teacher in this whole school.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Listen here, you little weasel—I've been teaching for twenty years and I know every trick in the book. Now sit down and do your homework.

jokes.text: Have a 'D'-lightful summer! [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Bart desperately needs good grades for camp
jokes.punchline: Teacher makes cruel pun about his failure
jokes.explanation: Solid pun delivery with character-appropriate cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: I'm afraid your grades just aren't good enough for camp, Bart.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Mrs. Krabappel, I really need to go!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Well, Bart, it looks like you won't be going to camp this summer. I guess you could say your dreams are... on the waiting list.

jokes.text: We won! Yea! U.S.A! U.S.A! U.S.A!
jokes.setup: Teacher asks how WWI ended
jokes.punchline: Students respond with mindless patriotic chanting
jokes.explanation: Good satire of American education and mindless patriotism

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: How did World War I end?

dialogue.speaker: Students
dialogue.line: USA! USA! USA!

jokes.text: I haven't seen such unfettered hurly-burly since the fall of Saigon.
jokes.setup: Students celebrating end of school
jokes.punchline: Skinner compares to traumatic war experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work referencing Skinner's Vietnam service with overwrought comparison

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Well, children, another school year has come to an end. I must say, this is almost as exciting as the day I was discharged from the Army.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Of course, that involved less screaming and considerably fewer casualties.

jokes.text: Make sure to give those toilets a good scrubbing. We want the old girls sparkling when I get back. Aye, sir. Eh, you silk-wearing buttercup!
jokes.setup: Skinner giving Willie cleaning instructions
jokes.punchline: Willie's bizarre insult after agreeing
jokes.explanation: Good Willie character moment with creative insult delivery

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Now Willie, I need you to clean out the gutters and trim the hedges.

dialogue.speaker: Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, no problem Mr. Skinner. I'll get right on it.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Excellent. Thank you, Willie.

dialogue.speaker: Willie
dialogue.line: Ye can take yer fancy mop and yer push broom and stick 'em where the sun don't shine, ye four-eyed sidewinder!

jokes.text: All right! Three whole months of Spaghetti Os and daytime TV!
jokes.setup: Summer vacation beginning
jokes.punchline: Bart's low-rent celebration plans
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Bart's simple pleasures

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ah man, summer vacation! No more school for three months.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'm gonna sleep till noon, eat nothing but junk food, and watch TV all day.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: This is gonna be the best summer ever!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Not so fast, boy. You're gonna help me fix the gutters.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Aw, man!

jokes.text: You bet. Check out this hand. All aces. [Laughing] A-plus!
jokes.setup: Milhouse asks if Bart will join him at camp
jokes.punchline: Shows report card as poker hand of aces
jokes.explanation: Good visual pun connecting to earlier setup about fake grades

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Hey Bart, you wanna go to camp with me?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Milhouse, look at this.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Four aces.

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Wow! That's an amazing poker hand!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: It's not a poker hand, man. It's my report card.

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, why didn't you at least forge plausible grades?
jokes.setup: Seeing Bart's obviously fake report card
jokes.punchline: Questions the execution, not the ethics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's practical approach to deception

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Dude, you can't just change your grades. The red ink is gonna like, totally give it away.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? This looks perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: Dude, it's in crayon.

jokes.text: Hey! Kamp Krusty is built on an actual Indian burial ground.
jokes.setup: Krusty advertising camp amenities
jokes.punchline: Presents horror movie trope as selling point
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of typical camp advertising with dark humor

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: At Krusty Kamp, we offer all the classic camp amenities: archery, canoeing, and best of all, a lake where a masked killer drowned fifty kids in 1957!

jokes.text: And for you fat kids... my exclusive program of diet and ridicule... will really get results.
jokes.setup: Krusty addressing overweight campers
jokes.punchline: Advertises cruelty as weight loss program
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures Krusty's callous commercialism

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Hey, hey! Listen up, you little chunk-a-lunks! I got a new weight loss program for ya. It's called 'Krusty's Cruelty Diet.' Every time you reach for a snack, I personally come to your house and mock you until you cry yourself to sleep! Hey, hey!

jokes.text: D'oh! Stupid roller skate.
jokes.setup: Homer stepping on roller skate
jokes.punchline: Classic slapstick with signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy but well-executed with Homer's reaction

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: You don't think much of me, do you, boy? No, sir. You know, a 'D' turns into a 'B' so easily. You just got greedy.
jokes.setup: Homer examining Bart's fake report card
jokes.punchline: Critiques the forgery technique, not the deception
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment showing his twisted parental wisdom and practical approach to crime

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hmm, you forged this pretty well, but you made one mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You used the wrong font. This is Comic Sans, and the school uses Times New Roman.

jokes.text: Now, Bart, we made this deal because I thought it would help you get good grades. And you didn't. But why should you pay for my mistake?
jokes.setup: Bart failing to meet the grade requirement
jokes.punchline: Homer takes responsibility in backwards way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - taking blame while completely missing the point

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I've failed as a father. I've failed as a husband. I've failed as an employee. But I've never failed as a man who takes responsibility for his mistakes.

jokes.text: I didn't want you hanging around all summer anyway.
jokes.setup: Homer allowing Bart to go to camp despite bad grades
jokes.punchline: Reveals selfish motivation behind kindness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer honesty undermining any appearance of good parenting

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sure, Bart, you can go to camp. I'm a pretty good father.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's very generous of you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, it gets him out of the house for eight weeks. That's two months of peace and quiet, no nagging, no 'Dad can I have this' or 'Dad can I have that.' Just me, you, and the couch.

jokes.text: Thanks, son. Now, you've got little hands. Can you reach under that mower and pull out that skate? [Engine Starts] Phew. Never mind.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Bart to help with stuck skate
jokes.punchline: Nearly gets Bart killed by starting mower
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalated danger and Homer's casual response to near-disaster

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Bart, help me get this skateboard unstuck from the lawnmower.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, I don't think that's a good idea.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't be such a baby. Just hold it steady while I start her up.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, no—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Here we go!

jokes.text: I'm gonna swim 'nekkid.' You're what? Ah, sure, there'll be a couple of up-tight counselors... who won't dig the Bart philosophy... but I feel the human body is a thing of beauty.
jokes.setup: Bart packing for camp without bathing suit
jokes.punchline: Delivers pseudo-intellectual justification for skinny-dipping
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character work with pretentious rationalization for mischief

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Look Mom, I'm not bringing a bathing suit to camp.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart! Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, I've been reading about the ancient Greeks, and they invented the Olympic Games where athletes competed completely nude. So really, swimming without clothes is actually a celebration of classical athletic tradition and the human form.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

jokes.text: Marge, am I crazy or is my back getting hairier?
jokes.setup: Family conversation about camp
jokes.punchline: Homer's random personal observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer non-sequitur but not particularly memorable

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Camp? Camp?! Why would I send my son to camp to sit around the campfire and write in a diary? I spent two weeks at fat camp and all I got out of it was this T-shirt and the realization that I have no business in shorts.

jokes.text: There's only one known cure-- a 'wowwipop.' [Chuckling] Don't patronize me, Doctor.
jokes.setup: Lisa diagnosed with hypochondria
jokes.punchline: Doctor's baby talk cure met with adult response
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics with Lisa's mature response to condescending treatment

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Now Lisa, I'm going to try a little baby talk to help you relax.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Goo goo ga ga!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Doctor, I'm eight years old. That's not going to work on me.

jokes.text: Hey, hands off my pickle! I don't see your name on it, boy. No, but-- Oh, yeah? Check... mate! Always thinking two moves ahead.
jokes.setup: Father-son dispute over pickle
jokes.punchline: Homer writes his name on pickle like chess victory
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment combining pettiness with unearned pride in 'strategy'

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, that's my pickle!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Not anymore, boy. I'm claiming this pickle as mine.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You can't just take my pickle!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Watch me.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm writing my name on it. There. Homer J. Simpson. Checkmate, Bart.

jokes.text: Spare me your euphemisms. It's fat camp for Daddy's chubby little secret! You promised you wouldn't make a scene.
jokes.setup: Mother using polite term for fat camp
jokes.punchline: Martin brutally honest about his situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with Martin's articulate self-awareness about his situation

dialogue.speaker: Martin's Mother
dialogue.line: Martin, we're sending you to a character-building camp for husky boys.

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: You mean fat camp. Let's not candy-coat it, Mother. I'm a blimp.

jokes.text: Remember: leaves of three, let it be. Leaves of four, eat some more. [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Giving Lisa safety advice about poison ivy
jokes.punchline: Adds nonsensical dangerous advice
jokes.explanation: Good Homer parenting fail with rhyming structure that sounds authoritative but isn't

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Now Lisa, poison ivy can cause a terrible rash. So remember: if you ever touch poison ivy, immediately rub it all over your body to spread it around evenly. That way the rash won't be lopsided.

jokes.text: Don't look in my closet. In fact, stay out of my room altogether. If the pets die, don't replace them. I'll know!
jokes.setup: Kids giving goodbye instructions to parents
jokes.punchline: Escalating paranoia and distrust
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing kids' distrust of their parents' competence

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Don't let Mom leave the house without checking her purse for stolen merchandise.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: And make sure Dad doesn't try to sell the TV again.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: If either of them acts suspicious, lock them in the basement.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Keep a gun hidden in case they try to escape.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Trust no one. Not even each other.

jokes.text: So long! Don't come back!
jokes.setup: Parents waving goodbye to camp bus
jokes.punchline: One parent's honest reaction
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway line revealing true parental feelings

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bye, honey! Have fun at camp!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, see you in eight weeks!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo!

jokes.text: I'll see you in a few weeks. Until then, I've turned things over... to my bestest buddy in the whole wide world-- [Dubbed Voice] Mr. Black. I want you to treat-- [Dubbed Voice] Mr. Black. with the same respect you would give me. Now here's-- [Dubbed Voice] Mr. Black.
jokes.setup: Krusty introducing camp director
jokes.punchline: Obviously dubbed video with repeated bad edits
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag showing cheap production and Krusty's corner-cutting

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: And here's our camp director, Mr. Black!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Hello children, I'm Mr. Black.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Hello children, I'm Mr. Black.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Hello children, I'm Mr. Black.

jokes.text: For the past 15 years, I was president of Euro-Krustyland... until it blew up.
jokes.setup: Mr. Black introducing his credentials
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions catastrophic failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery of alarming information as resume enhancement

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: I'm Mr. Black, head of Western Segment of the International Lemon Tree Cartel.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: We've had a small problem with quality control, but it's nothing we can't handle.

jokes.text: Can we call you Uncle Blackie? No. Last question. When do we get to see Krusty? Uh, he will be along eventually.
jokes.setup: Kids asking innocent questions
jokes.punchline: Authority figure being evasive and controlling
jokes.explanation: Good setup for camp's authoritarian nature with Mr. Black's controlling responses

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Why can't we go outside?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Because it's dangerous out there.

dialogue.speaker: Another Child
dialogue.line: But you said that yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Yes, well, it's still dangerous. Now stop asking questions.

jokes.text: In the meantime, our counselors Dolph, Jimbo and Kearney... will be happy to handle any problems you may have.
jokes.setup: Introducing camp counselors
jokes.punchline: They're clearly bullies, not caregivers
jokes.explanation: Good ironic casting of known bullies as 'helpful' counselors

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: They're clearly bullies, not caregivers

jokes.text: Looks like we got ourselves a troublemaker. [Grunting]
jokes.setup: Kid coughs during orientation
jokes.punchline: Brutal overreaction to minor disruption
jokes.explanation: Standard bully behavior establishing camp's harsh atmosphere

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: Who coughed? WHO COUGHED?

dialogue.speaker: Dolph
dialogue.line: It was just a little cough, man.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: A LITTLE COUGH?! That's it! You're all expelled! School is CLOSED! Permanently!

jokes.text: Here's your cabin. If you don't like it, T.S.
jokes.setup: Showing kids their accommodations
jokes.punchline: Hostile attitude with mild profanity substitute
jokes.explanation: Basic establishment of counselor hostility with period-appropriate euphemism

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Welcome to Camp! Here's where you'll be sleeping.

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Now, I don't want to hear any of that badgering crap.

jokes.text: This is a little more rustic than I expected. I'm not worried, Lise. You know why? Because of this. The Krusty Brand Seal of Approval.
jokes.setup: Terrible cabin conditions
jokes.punchline: Bart trusts in meaningless brand seal
jokes.explanation: Good satire of brand loyalty and consumer trust in meaningless endorsements

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, this cabin is disgusting. The plumbing doesn't work, there's mold everywhere, and I'm pretty sure that's a family of raccoons living in the walls.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Relax, man. See this? It says 'Good Housekeeping Seal of Approval.' We're gonna be fine.

jokes.text: Oops. I should've warned you. That clock gets incredibly hot if you leave it plugged in.
jokes.setup: Kid gets burned touching clock
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of known dangerous defect
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about negligent safety with casual delivery

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Well, we've known about that clock for years.

jokes.text: Don't we get to roast marshmallows? Shut up and eat your pinecone.
jokes.setup: Kid asking about normal camp activity
jokes.punchline: Forced to eat inedible natural objects
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between camp expectations and harsh reality with memorable punchline

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: What do we do at camp?

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Well, we do a lot of outdoor activities. Today we're going to forage for our lunch.

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Forage? You mean like pick berries and nuts?

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Sure, berries, nuts, bark, leaves, whatever we can find.

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Bark? Leaves?

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: It's all edible if you believe hard enough.

jokes.text: Well, it ain't gettin' any safer.
jokes.setup: Kid questioning safety of dangerous activity
jokes.punchline: Counselor's fatalistic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of words with dark humor about safety negligence

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: But isn't that dangerous?

dialogue.speaker: Counselor Skinner
dialogue.line: Oh yes, very dangerous. But you know, we all have to die of something.

jokes.text: All right, you balls of pan drippings! I want to see Crisco coming out of those pores! We're not leaving until this Christmas ham gives me a pull-up.
jokes.setup: Harsh fitness instruction at fat camp
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate food-based insults
jokes.explanation: Creative and cruel fat camp instructor dialogue with memorable food metaphors

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: You blubbery idiot! You're nothing but a walking advertisement for fried chicken!

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: You disgusting sack of hamburger meat and mayonnaise!

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: You're a festering pile of donuts, Twinkies, and everything that's wrong with America's diet!

jokes.text: You're serving us gruel? Not quite. This is Krusty Brand Imitation Gruel. Nine out of 10 orphans can't tell the difference.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about terrible food
jokes.punchline: Even worse - fake version tested on orphans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with dark humor about corporate cost-cutting and orphan testing

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: This food is terrible!

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Well, it could be worse. This is actually the real version. We test the fake version on orphans.

jokes.text: Yo, Mr. Black. Another brandy. Gentlemen, to evil.
jokes.setup: Camp staff relaxing while kids suffer
jokes.punchline: Explicitly toasting their villainy
jokes.explanation: Good villain moment with openly acknowledging their evil nature

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Here's to us!

dialogue.speaker: Counselors
dialogue.line: To us!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: And to the kids suffering out there in the woods.

dialogue.speaker: Counselors
dialogue.line: To the kids!

jokes.text: Marge, since the kids left, I've lost five pounds! Oh, that's wonderful! And look-- new hair! I'm this close to having a comb-over.
jokes.setup: Homer showing off weight loss
jokes.punchline: Proud of pathetic amount of new hair
jokes.explanation: Good Homer vanity moment with realistic male pride in minimal progress

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Look at me! I lost weight!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's wonderful!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: And look, I'm growing back some hair!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's just a few strands, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know! Aren't they magnificent?

jokes.text: I feel like I'm gonna die, Bart. We're all gonna die, Lise. I meant soon. So did I.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing fear about camp conditions
jokes.punchline: Bart confirming immediate mortal danger
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation with philosophical/immediate danger contrast

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm scared, Bart. What if the camp conditions are terrible?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Don't worry, sis. We're in immediate mortal danger.

jokes.text: Hey, Lendl! Choke! Choke! Yeah! [Man] Point and game, Becker. And will the harlequin in the third row Please keep his voice down? [Chortles] Sorry, folks.
jokes.setup: Krusty watching tennis at Wimbledon
jokes.punchline: Being obnoxious and getting called out
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty character work showing his crassness in refined setting

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: And that's match point to Federer!

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey! Over here! Take a picture of me!

dialogue.speaker: Official
dialogue.line: Sir, please be quiet. You're disrupting the match.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Disrupting? I'm enhancing! This is comedy gold!

dialogue.speaker: Official
dialogue.line: You need to leave immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: What? But I paid good money for this seat!

jokes.text: Hey, Simpson! Tell your mom her cookies sucked.
jokes.setup: Bart receiving care package from home
jokes.punchline: Counselor's unnecessary cruelty about gift
jokes.explanation: Basic cruelty establishing counselor hostility to family connections

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Well, well, well. A care package from home. How... quaint. I suppose your parents felt obligated to send you something, even though we both know they'd rather forget you exist.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, man, that's not cool.

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Not cool? Let me tell you what's not cool, Simpson. Those cookies your mother baked are stale. Your father probably sat on them during the drive to the post office.

jokes.text: Dear Mom and Dad, I no longer fear hell... because I've been to Kamp Krusty.
jokes.setup: Lisa writing letter home from camp
jokes.punchline: Compares camp to eternal damnation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa line combining her intelligence with dramatic comparison - highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dear Mom and Dad, Camp is wonderful, though I must say the conditions here bear a striking resemblance to Dante's nine circles of Hell. The food is inedible, the counselors are sadistic, and I'm fairly certain one of the other campers is actually Satan himself. Yours in eternal damnation, Lisa.

jokes.text: A snake bit me. Back in line, maggot!
jokes.setup: Kid reporting serious injury during hike
jokes.punchline: Dismissed with military cruelty
jokes.explanation: Standard dark humor about negligent medical care with military styling

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: I broke my leg on the hike!

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Walk it off, soldier.

jokes.text: Come on, wimps! These Gucci wallets have to be on the streets of Hong Kong by Friday.
jokes.setup: Arts and crafts activity at camp
jokes.punchline: Revealed as illegal sweatshop labor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation turning innocent craft time into child labor exploitation

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Alright campers, time for arts and crafts! You'll be making license plates.

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Now, I don't want to know where they go or what happens to them. Just make 'em.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, isn't this illegal sweatshop labor?

dialogue.speaker: Counselor
dialogue.line: Did I stutter, Simpson? Make the plates!

jokes.text: Krusty is coming. Krusty is coming. Krusty's coming. But I am far more pessimistic.
jokes.setup: Bart maintaining faith in Krusty's arrival
jokes.punchline: Lisa's characteristically rational despair
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast with Bart's optimism vs Lisa's realism

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: He'll be here, Lisa. Krusty never lets me down.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, he's let you down approximately 600 times. The probability of him showing up is statistically insignificant.

jokes.text: Ah, kids' letters from camp. Oh, she complains now, but when we go to pick her up, she won't want to leave.
jokes.setup: Reading Lisa's desperate letter about camp conditions
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misreading the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness contrasting with audience knowledge of true conditions

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Dear Dad, Camp is a nightmare. The food is slop. I'm homesick. Please get me out of here.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, we have to go get her!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, relax. She's just being dramatic. Besides, look at this P.S. — 'P.S. I'm having the time of my life!' She's fine!

jokes.text: Lisa, I've been thinking it over. Next summer, I'm getting a job. [Moaning]
jokes.setup: Terrible camp conditions continuing
jokes.punchline: Bart prefers child labor to returning to camp
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how bad camp is that work seems preferable to Bart

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You know, I'd rather work in a sweatshop than go back to that camp.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, that's ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'm serious. At least they give you bathroom breaks.

jokes.text: Marge, we've got all the fireworks we need right here.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to go see fireworks
jokes.punchline: Homer's romantic/sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy innuendo from Homer

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, let's go see the fireworks.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fireworks? Marge, the only fireworks I need are the ones going off inside my pants.

jokes.text: See? I told you Krusty would come. Just like I said. He's gonna bring us food and water, and smite our enemies!
jokes.setup: Krusty finally appearing at camp
jokes.punchline: Bart's messianic expectations for celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bart's faith with biblical language about celebrity salvation

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Krusty! You came!

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, of course I came. What'd ya think, I'm gonna miss a chance to see a bunch of kids?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: We've been waiting for you all summer. We even built a shrine.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: A shrine? For me? Aw, that's sweet, kid.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: We thought you'd cure our diseases, turn water into Squishees, maybe walk on the lake.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Walk on the lake? Kid, I can barely walk on land!

jokes.text: Now, I must tell you kids, Krusty has laryngitis and a bad back... so he won't be saying anything or doing anything. Krusty looks fat. He's really having trouble keeping his balance. He's still funny, but not ha-ha funny.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Krusty isn't performing
jokes.punchline: Kids observing obvious impostor
jokes.explanation: Good setup with kids' innocent but accurate observations about the fake Krusty

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Krusty the Clown is indisposed today, so I'll be filling in for him.

dialogue.speaker: Children
dialogue.line: You're not Krusty!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: No, but I'm a trained actor.

dialogue.speaker: Children
dialogue.line: You don't even have a big red nose!

jokes.text: That's not Krusty the Clown! [Kids Gasping] What do you think? I slapped a clown suit on some wino? [Nervous Laughing] I mean, I-- Yeah, Bart. I am so Krunchy the Clown! [Belches]
jokes.setup: Bart calling out the obvious fake
jokes.punchline: Mr. Black accidentally admits to using homeless person
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal with accidental confession and pathetic impostor performance

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait a minute, you're not the real Krusty the Clown!

dialogue.speaker: Fake Krusty
dialogue.line: Yes I am!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: No you're not. I can tell by your face.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Black
dialogue.line: Well, we would have gotten away with it too, if it weren't for that meddling kid. We found this guy sleeping behind a dumpster.

jokes.text: All right. That's it. I've been scorched by Krusty before. I got a rapid heartbeat from those Krusty Brand vitamins. My Krusty calculator didn't have a seven or an eight! And Krusty's autobiography was self-serving with many glaring omissions. But this time, he's gone too far!
jokes.setup: Bart's breaking point with fake Krusty
jokes.punchline: Catalog of past Krusty merchandise failures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating rant with specific absurd product failures building to righteous anger

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Remember the Krusty Komedy Klassics VHS set that got recalled because it was mislabeled as a Kidz Bop compilation?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Or the Krusty action figure with the spring-loaded pants that kept launching kids across playgrounds?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: How about the Krusty lunchbox that turned every sandwich into mystery meat within hours?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: The Krusty skateboard that only turned left?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: The Krusty alarm clock that screamed instead of rang?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: And let's not forget the Krusty cologne that attracted wasps instead of dates!

jokes.text: We want Krusty! We want Krusty! We want Krusty! Yeah, we want Krunchy! We want Krunchy!
jokes.setup: Kids chanting for real Krusty
jokes.punchline: Fake Krusty joins in with wrong name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comedy with impostor's clueless participation

dialogue.speaker: Children
dialogue.line: Krusty! Krusty! Krusty!

dialogue.speaker: Fake Krusty
dialogue.line: Crusty! Crusty! Crusty!

jokes.text: My chunky brothers, gorge yourselves at the trough of freedom! [Cheering] [Slurping] Ahh! Sweet, nourishing gruel!
jokes.setup: Bart liberating the camp food supply
jokes.punchline: Gruel becomes feast after starvation
jokes.explanation: Good revolution moment with ironic appreciation for previously despised food

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, check it out! I liberated the camp food supply!

dialogue.speaker: Children
dialogue.line: Gruel? This is gruel!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, but after three weeks of starvation, it's a feast!

jokes.text: Kowalski! My brownies! Wiggum! A change of underwear! Crandall! My insulin!
jokes.setup: Bart organizing liberated campers
jokes.punchline: Mix of treats and medical necessities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from trivial to serious needs showing camp's complete neglect

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Alright campers, listen up! We've got treats and medical supplies to distribute.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Who needs s'mores? Who needs band-aids? Step right up!

jokes.text: Bart! You said you were gonna name it Camp Freedom! Aah, this has more zing. I dub thee Sir-- Urgent call for Mr. Clown.
jokes.setup: Bart renaming the liberated camp
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by urgent call disrupting ceremony
jokes.explanation: Decent transition moment with Bart's preference for dramatic naming

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: And so I hereby declare this camp to be renamed... Camp...

dialogue.speaker: Phone
dialogue.line: RING RING!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Aw, man!

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, I've been to Vietnam, Afghanistan and Iraq... and I can say without hyperbole... that this is a million times worse than all of them put together.
jokes.setup: Serious war correspondent providing context
jokes.punchline: Absurd overstatement about summer camp
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd comparison with professional war correspondent's perspective on camp conditions

jokes.text: [Thinking] Don't be the boy. Don't be the boy. D'oh!
jokes.setup: News about to interview camp ringleader
jokes.punchline: Homer's prayer immediately answered negatively
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment with immediate dashed hopes and signature reaction

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Dear God, please let them not ask about the time I let Bart fall in the well.

dialogue.speaker: News Reporter
dialogue.line: So, about the time you let Bart fall in the well...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: I just want the whole world to know that this was a really crappy camp. Can I say 'crappy' on TV? Yes, on this network, you can.
jokes.setup: Bart giving TV interview about camp
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about Fox network standards
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about network's permissive content standards

jokes.text: Is it true you attempted suicide when you heard the news? Are you and Princess Di just friends? You people make me sick! You're vultures! Where were you when I sang at Farm Aid? Out of my way, you parasites! I said, out of my way!
jokes.setup: Reporters asking intrusive questions
jokes.punchline: Krusty's increasingly unhinged responses
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing media circus with Krusty's celebrity meltdown

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.line: Mr. the Clown, is it true you've been avoiding taxes?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: That's a lie! I pay my taxes... to a guy named Vinny in Jersey.

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.line: And what about your numerous affairs?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Affairs? Lady, I'm a lover, not a fighter. Although I've been both, sometimes simultaneously.

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.line: Do you have any regrets?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Regrets? Kid, I regret nothing! Except that time I invested my money in a pillow factory. Worst decision ever - I was always sleeping on the job!

jokes.text: I'm no fake. I'm the real Krusty. Oh, yeah? Who played your daughter in the short-lived sitcom President Clown? I don't know her name, but she held up a liquor store last year.
jokes.setup: Krusty proving his identity with trivia question
jokes.punchline: Answers with tragic celebrity child story
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about child star tragedy serving as identity verification

dialogue.speaker: Reporter
dialogue.line: What's the name of Krusty's son?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Mertz. But everybody thinks he's dead because of that time I drove him out to the woods and left him there. Course, he came back.

jokes.text: I smell bacon. Let's see if he's wearing a wire! Hey! Whoa! No! Not the face! [Gasping] It is the real Krusty. Look at that pacemaker scar, the cattle skull birthmark... and his famous superfluous nipple.
jokes.setup: Kids checking if Krusty is police informant
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and gross identifying features
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of increasingly bizarre celebrity body features

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Is Krusty a police informant?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: No, no, no! I'm not a rat!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, you do have that scar on your left cheek, the glass eye, the wooden leg, and that spot where your skin is rotting away from all those years of smoking...

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Hey! Those are just identifying features!

jokes.text: At least you're not as bad as Customs. How could you, Krusty? I'd never lend my name to an inferior product. [Loud Sobbing] Oh! They drove a dump truck full of money up to my house. I'm not made of stone!
jokes.setup: Lisa confronting Krusty about camp conditions
jokes.punchline: Krusty's honest confession about being bought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - admits to principles but explains realistic corruption

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Krusty, how could you let the camp conditions get so bad?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Look kid, I'm gonna level with ya. I was bought.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bought? By who?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: By the camp! They paid me to put my name on it and look the other way. I don't even go there!

jokes.text: Krusty, this camp was a nightmare. They fed us gruel. They forced us to make wallets for export. And one of the campers was eaten by a bear. Oh, my God! [Sobbing] Well, actually, the bear just ate his hat. Was it a nice hat? Oh, yeah. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Lisa listing camp horrors including bear attack
jokes.punchline: Clarification makes it less serious, then more important again
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection with Krusty's misplaced priorities about hat quality over child safety

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A bear attack, but it turned out to be Krusty in a bear suit.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Hey, I needed the work!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But then the suit fell off and it was a real bear.

jokes.text: I'm gonna make it up to you. I'm gonna show you kids the time of your life. Get ready for two weeks at the Happiest Place on Earth-- Tijuana! [Cheering]
jokes.setup: Krusty promising to make amends with great destination
jokes.punchline: Takes kids to seedy Mexican border town
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty conclusion - subverts Disney expectation with inappropriate adult destination that kids love

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: I'm gonna make it up to you kids! We're going to the most magical, wonderful place on Earth!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Really? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: We're going to... Tijuana!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Dad, isn't that a seedy border town?

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: Seedy? Kid, it's got character!
